subject: WOW!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Greitens for Missouri, Jeff Stuerman, Treasurer
body:
Peter,

WOW! Eric had his first debate today against career politician Chris Koster. The difference between the two could not have been more clear. Eric offered common-sense conservative ideas to fix Missouri's problems. Career politician Chris Koster offered more big government solutions just like Hillary Clinton. KOSTER'S TOP PRIORITY is EXPANDING OBAMACARE in Missouri!

The polls are close and we need to raise $23,650 by midnight tonight to meet our monthly goal. By chipping in [$100, $50, $25 or whatever you can](http://ericgreitens.us13.list-manage2.com/track/click?u=c81357bdb59fb170d9eb82b3a&id=2c96881272&e=84041254e6), your support will make sure we keep our momentum going.

Your generous donation today will keep Clinton-style, career politicians like Chris Koster out of the governor's office.

On to victory,
AC

Austin Chambers
Campaign Manager
Paid for by Greitens for Missouri, Jeff Stuerman, Treasurer
4579 Laclede Avenue #138
St. Louis, Missouri 63108
Eric is a former member of the United States Navy having served four deployments overseas. Use of his military rank, titles and photographs in uniform does not imply an endorsement from the Department of the Navy or the Department of Defense.
This email was sent to dpwillis67+greitens@gmail.com. You can also follow us on [Facebook](http://ericgreitens.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=c81357bdb59fb170d9eb82b3a&id=3321afc6bc&e=84041254e6) and [Twitter](http://ericgreitens.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=c81357bdb59fb170d9eb82b3a&id=72f061f772&e=84041254e6). Emails are one of the best ways to stay connected with the campaign. If they become too much, you can [unsubscribe](http://ericgreitens.us13.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=c81357bdb59fb170d9eb82b3a&id=3f71c40094&e=84041254e6&c=aafdebe465), but please let us know how we can improve. You can always reach us at [team@ericgreitens.com](mailto:team@ericgreitens.com) or (314) 899-0288. You're still reading the fineprint? You must be a detail oriented, all-around awesome person who is a big supporter — you should [volunteer](http://ericgreitens.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=c81357bdb59fb170d9eb82b3a&id=3a6fa2c874&e=84041254e6) with us!

Donald J. Trump

body:
| | | Phil Scott's Week In Action
---
[View this email in your browser](http://us13.campaign-archive2.com/?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=cca0b6fde8&e=19cebfe173)
---
| |
---
| | | | | | [](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=582162634a&e=19cebfe173) | [Share](http://philscott.us13.list-manage2.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=a1abc38534&e=19cebfe173)

| Greetings,

| Another great week on the campaign trail is in the books, as Vermonters continue to show enthusiastic support for Phil’s economic ideas and message. Check out this week’s highlights from the state’s most energetic and hardworking campaign.

**EARLY VOTING HAS BEGUN, ALONG WITH _GOTV_ EFFORTS**
Early voting has begun! Don't want to wait until November 8 to vote for Phil? Click [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=e5e593387b&e=19cebfe173) to find your town clerk's location or to request an absentee ballot. This election is important. If you’re committed to voting for Phil – and we hope you are because **it’s time for a leader focused on the economy** – get to the polls early to help ensure he’s elected on November 8.

Getting out the vote for the election is critical – we want all Vermonters’ voices to be heard. To support these efforts for Republican candidates throughout the state, Phil will be at the **Vermont Republican Party 2016 Fall Harvest Dinner** on Wednesday, October 5. **This event helps raise resources for _Get Out the Vote_ efforts** for the November 8 election. RSVP today by clicking [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=cee44a7ed2&e=19cebfe173).

**PHIL, A LEADER OF INTEGRITY, CONTINUING TO WORK FOR YOU**
At a party celebrating the 70th anniversary of Dubois Construction, Phil announced that **he will sell his share in the company if he is elected the next Governor of Vermont.**

In his remarks, Phil shared his personal story and history with Dubois Construction, the “ups and downs,” highlighting some of the challenges of owning a business and how a small, family-run business must tackle adversity in order to survive. **These are lessons that have informed his leadership style and give him the kind of _perspective we need in Montpelier_**.

“Though I would have never guessed it 16 years ago, I have come to understand that this work — the work of making sure the voice of every working Vermonter and every family is heard in Montpelier — is never done,” he said. “I know this decision is the right one and **will assure Vermonters that _there is nothing more important to me than serving them_ and following through on the commitments I am making in this campaign _to make the economy and affordability Montpelier’s top priorities_** , and to restore the faith and trust that has been lost in state government in the last six years.”

Having spent 30 years growing this successful small business, this was a tough decision for Phil. **But that is what leadership is about. This is the type of leadership we need in the Governor’s office!** Read more [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=f69acd3788&e=19cebfe173).

Photo Credit: @PhilScott4VT

***
**HEALTHCARE REFORM ESSENTIAL TO ECONOMIC & AFFORDABILITY GOALS**
Phil toured Springfield Hospital Tuesday and presented his healthcare reform priorities as part of a Dartmouth-Hitchcock Medical Center policy series. You can view Phil’s plan to get healthcare reform back on track [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=d246a4d9a9&e=19cebfe173). As he told the group, **addressing our healthcare challenges is central to his goals of strengthening the economy and making Vermont more affordable**.

Making progress requires **setting aside ideological crusades and working together to craft a system that is more efficient, innovative and can care for everyone**. Phil’s plan includes moving to a functional health insurance exchange with more affordable options, while preserving subsidies; getting Medicaid and long-term care costs under control; enforcing full price transparency; and changing federal laws to allow Vermonters to purchase insurance across state lines. Read more about the Springfield Hospital event in _Vermont Business_ [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=50dbff60de&e=19cebfe173).

Photo Credit: @DartmouthHitch

***
**THIS WEEK’S DEBATES AND FORUMS HIGHLIGHT FUNDAMENTAL DIFFERENCES**
Phil participated in a number of forums and debates this week, showcasing why he is **the only candidate that is committed to moving Vermont forward _without_ adding to Vermont’s crisis of affordability**.

There is a fundamental difference in how Phil and his opponent would approach Vermont’s future. We’ve seen the result of six years of unsustainable budgeting practices that spend beyond our means and get distracted by pet projects and ideological debates. That’s why “focusing on the economy and affordability” isn’t just a talking point, Phil means it. As Governor, **Phil will work to keep Montpelier focused on the fundamentals of building a dynamic economy**. He knows if we all work together, focus on common ground, and commit to the goal of making Vermont more prosperous, that **we can make Vermont more affordable for working families, balance the budget,_and_ support our most vulnerable**.

That vision and leadership was demonstrated in a number of debates this week. As those debates illustrated, there is a clear contrast between Phil and his opponent, including what their priorities would be, and **whether we’ll balance the budget by increasing taxes and fees on working families and businesses, or through modernization, efficiency and organic growth from a more robust economy.**

If you weren’t able to join us at the debates this week, check out the Digger Dialogue [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=f5f6460cc1&e=19cebfe173).

Photo Credit: @Thunder_Truck

***
**TEAM SCOTT IS IN FULL GEAR**
With just over five weeks to go in this election, Phil is working hard to meet with as many Vermonters as he can, so he understands your challenges and how he can best serve you as Governor. This week, he shared his plans and priorities with industry professionals, advocates, businesses and families during forums at **Springfield Hospital** , the **Prevent Child Abuse Conference** , and the **Homebuilders Breakfast at Snyder Homes**. Phil and Team Scott also enjoyed a pig roast in **Charlotte** , the **Bristol Village Festival** , **Underhill’s Harvest Market Festival parade** and met with Vermonters in **Fairfax** and at **Wheel House Designs in Morristown**. Thanks to everyone who is participating our events!

Photo Credit: @PhilScottVT
Stay up-to-date on our upcoming events [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=4b78d43b45&e=19cebfe173). We hope to see you at our many events next week, including the [American Academy of Pediatrics Vermont Chapter Q&A](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=7599da6018&e=19cebfe173), [Permanent Fund for Vermont’s Children](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=d95a7d0463&e=19cebfe173), the [Jet service BBQ](http://philscott.us13.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=0b57d601e0&e=19cebfe173), [Vermont’s Council of Developmental and Mental Health Services discussion](http://philscott.us13.list-manage2.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=4b216131e8&e=19cebfe173) and the [Vermont Technology Council Breakfast](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=24e936ba6c&e=19cebfe173).
***
**ICYMI: PHIL IN THE NEWS**

**_Scott, Brock are top choices_**
“Just compare the qualifications and administrative experiences of Phil Scott and Randy Brock against their opponents, both with very limited administrative experience. I think you will agree who should be our next governor and lieutenant governor.” – Chet Greenwood, Derby (Source: Rutland Herald: [Full Letter](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=5fe77f6441&e=19cebfe173))

**_Democrats for Phil_**
“As a lifelong Democrat/Independent, I have voted for Phil in the past because of his integrity. Many members of the Democratic party have lost their integrity and do not care about Vermonter’s health and welfare.” Bruce and Sally Collopy, Fairfield (Source: Caledonian Record: [Full Letter](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=364ccc902e&e=19cebfe173))
_If you’re a Democrat who, like Bruce and Sally, plans to vote for Phil, we’d love to hear more. Please email[miranda@philscott.org](mailto:miranda@philscott.org)_

**_Phil’s non-divisive style and thoughtful approach appeals across party lines_**
_“_ There is, after all, considerable overlap in the appeal of Republican Phil Scott and democratic socialist Bernie Sanders: ‘The reason he’s popular is he’s different than the national party,’ says [WDEV’s Mike Smith]. That is the Vermont way.” (Source: Politico: [Full Article](http://philscott.us13.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=f22905f414&e=19cebfe173))

**_Phil: Prioritize spending on people, not processes_**
“Prevention of child abuse is crucial, and finding ways to redirect dollars away from the process and to the people who are really actually in need requires constant, constant focus on improving the operation performance of every single agency.” (Source: VT Digger: [Full Article](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=a77fcd3d91&e=19cebfe173))

**_Phil: Give Vermonters a voice in healthcare_**
“Vermonters deserve to have a really detailed discussion about [Gov. Shumlin’s plans for an all-payer healthcare model] before we move forward. And if that takes a month, two months, six months or a year, I think it's worth having. So I think it's premature to lock us into an agreement, something that the next administration will have to deal with." (Source: Vermont Public Radio: [Full Article](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=1ee39fe4d7&e=19cebfe173))

**_In defense of democracy_**
“Phil Scott is the person defending democratic traditions in this case.” (Source: Tom Koch in VT Digger: [Full Commentary](http://philscott.us13.list-manage2.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=c8c2c2f53f&e=19cebfe173))
***
**ICYMI: THIS WEEK’S #GOODREADS**

**_Phil: A plan for a more prosperous future_**
In an opinion piece contributed to the _Rutland Herald_ this week, **Phil outlines his plans for economic growth, and how he’ll work to make Vermont more affordable as Governor**. As he states in the article, “To take Vermont’s economy in a more prosperous direction, it is essential that our state leadership and legislators focus on economic growth and making Vermont more affordable for families and businesses.” Learn more about Phil’s strategy for growth by reading his full article [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=9933ab35e7&e=19cebfe173) and his economic plans [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=cb2e7ab559&e=19cebfe173).

**_John Klar: Watch the birdie politics_**
John Klar is a Vermont grass-fed beef and sheep farmer, and an attorney and pastor. And he is fed up with candidates who seek to distract Vermonters from our real economic challenges by bringing up partisan issues that aren’t a priority here in Vermont. As he writes in _VT Digger_ , “We have a bit of a crisis unfolding in Vermont. **Our state is limited by the realities of the economy, while our government has in recent years overspent, overtaxed and wasted (Vermont Health Connect) our financial resources**.” Klar explains how our current leadership has led us down this road and notes, “**The one thing Democrats do not want discussed come November is fiscal accountability. Vermonters are still feeling the sting of recent fee and tax increases** : Democrats have little to no credibility proposing a fiscal solution to our current plight.” Instead, he writes, the Democrats running for Vermont’s top jobs are working to distract Vermonters. “**Maybe you accept lots of out-of-state money to shift the debate to guns and abortions, neither of which is a real issue for struggling Vermonters, but both of which give candidates two things – easy money and a hot-button issue to create that ‘little birdie’ to distract voters from that pesky budget thing**. Democrats are holding a losing hand on fiscal responsibility – so they seek to garner a new hand of cards.” It’s an interesting perspective. We encourage you to read the full article [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=9b2ed32955&e=19cebfe173).

**John McClaughry: Tax Prospects for 2017**
Phil understands that Vermonters cannot afford more taxes and fees, and that we must end the unsustainable practice of “balancing" the budget on the backs of taxpayers! In an opinion piece in the _Rutland Herald,_ John McClaughry, vice president of the Ethan Allen Institute, outlines the tax battles that will be on the table for the new legislature and Governor. Read the full article [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=b6fb99359a&e=19cebfe173). **Phil is the only candidate who has committed to addressing our crisis of affordability, and who will not add new taxes and fees for Vermonters and businesses**. Read more on Phil’s economic plans [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=e76cc3d8ce&e=19cebfe173).
*******
Thanks to everyone for continuing to follow and support our campaign. Be sure to follow along with the campaign’s activities every day – and share your thoughts – [@PhilScott4VT](http://philscott.us13.list-manage2.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=09dcccca3d&e=19cebfe173) and [https://www.facebook.com/PhilScottforVermont/](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=af86d7a912&e=19cebfe173).

Sincerely,
Team Scott
---

46 Three Mile Bridge Road, Middlesex, VT 05602

Want to change how you receive these emails?
You can [update your preferences](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/profile?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=75128fb49c&e=19cebfe173) or [unsubscribe from this list](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=75128fb49c&e=19cebfe173&c=cca0b6fde8)

name: eric@holcombforindiana.com
subject: Fwd: Indiana is in good hands with Eric
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Eric Holcomb for Indiana
body:
I hope you had a chance to read Governor Pence’s email from earlier. I am honored that he has put his faith in me and my team to continue Indiana’s m | | | | | Friend, **I hope you had a chance to read Governor Pence’s email from earlier.** I am honored that he has put his faith in me and my team to continue Indiana’s momentum on jobs, taxes, and an overall improved Indiana. I just got off the phone with my finance team, and we’re just over a thousand dollars short from beating our $50,000 3rd quarter fundraising goal. **I wouldn’t be reaching out this late if it wasn’t important, but[will you contribute any amount to help us beat our goal?](http://links.holcombforindiana.com/u/7477d4a3aee24cde82b7298b32a84653?_e=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&_m=495dadd13ce74ab99e95c09ef67ce96c&utm_source=Iterable&utm_medium=text_header_cta_1&utm_term=Fundraising_Deadline&utm_campaign=EOM&utm_content=20160930-fwd-indiana-is-in-good-hands-with-eric-16266-direct)** **We only have until midnight tonight and, with you by my side, I know we can do it!** **[Contribute any amount to help us win!](http://links.holcombforindiana.com/u/312b397ab703452d8ad726fe7fba92f9?_e=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&_m=495dadd13ce74ab99e95c09ef67ce96c&utm_source=Iterable&utm_medium=text_header_cta_2&utm_term=Fundraising_Deadline&utm_campaign=EOM&utm_content=20160930-fwd-indiana-is-in-good-hands-with-eric-16266-direct)** Full Steam Ahead!Eric _\-- Sent from my iPad_ On Fri, Sep 30, 2016 at 6:15 AM, Mike Pence <[communications@holcombforindiana.com](http://links.holcombforindiana.com/u/a702f1d8c7df4219baa6baa4947ba1fe?_e=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&_m=495dadd13ce74ab99e95c09ef67ce96c&utm_source=Iterable&utm_medium=text_header_email&utm_term=Fundraising_Deadline&utm_campaign=EOM&utm_content=20160930-fwd-indiana-is-in-good-hands-with-eric-16266-direct)>
---
|
---
[ ](http://links.holcombforindiana.com/u/e94bd5acf5a74410925c0d2a5f177e33?_e=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&_m=495dadd13ce74ab99e95c09ef67ce96c&utm_source=Iterable&utm_medium=image_header_logo&utm_term=Fundraising_Deadline&utm_campaign=EOM&utm_content=20160930-fwd-indiana-is-in-good-hands-with-eric-16266-direct)
---
|
---
| | | | Friend, When I began my work to bring Hoosier values to the White House, I knew I was leaving Indiana in good hands with Eric Holcomb as your next Governor. Eric Holcomb is a proven consensus builder with a strong track record of getting commonsense, conservative results that move Indiana forward. Eric can turn ideas into solutions, and solutions into progress. As a veteran of the United States Navy and as a trusted advisor not only to me, but to Governor Daniels and Senator Coats, Eric has always answered the call. _**Now he needs you to answer that same call.**_ **Will you step up for my good friend Eric and help him become the next Governor?[Contribute to Team Holcomb today.](http://links.holcombforindiana.com/u/6b125ec8956e4014891edff7e400e26a?_e=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&_m=495dadd13ce74ab99e95c09ef67ce96c&utm_source=Iterable&utm_medium=text_body_cta_1&utm_term=Fundraising_Deadline&utm_campaign=EOM&utm_content=20160930-fwd-indiana-is-in-good-hands-with-eric-16266-direct)** **At midnight tonight, they have to close the books on their 3rd quarter fundraising – and they’re still short of their $50,000 goal.** I am honored to serve as Indiana’s Governor and I know that Eric will keep us on the path forward with principled, conservative solutions that help Indiana grow and prosper. **[Contribute any amount up to $2,700 and help them beat this goal and win in November.](http://links.holcombforindiana.com/u/610e363dfb124d53afb8ca0301632171?_e=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&_m=495dadd13ce74ab99e95c09ef67ce96c&utm_source=Iterable&utm_medium=text_body_cta_2&utm_term=Fundraising_Deadline&utm_campaign=EOM&utm_content=20160930-fwd-indiana-is-in-good-hands-with-eric-16266-direct)** Thank you,Mike Pence
---

name: Team Murphy
subject: everything is on the line
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Patrick Murphy | 4521 PGA Blvd #412, Palm Beach Gardens, FL 33418 | [http://murphyforflorida
body:
| |
---
| | [](https://secure.murphyforflorida.com/page/m/10ae2d9f/738133e4/311f72c7/7f857af5/1353111158/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/pme160930pm2?refcode=pme160930pm2_m&recur_weekly=true&amount=10")

| Team, tonight's the night. It's our final End-of-Quarter deadline of the entire campaign. Everything is on the line, and we only have 8 hours left. [**Give now to help us meet our $200,000 goal >>**](https://secure.murphyforflorida.com/page/m/10ae2d9f/738133e4/311f72c7/7f857af5/1353111158/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/pme160930pm2?refcode=pme160930pm2_m&recur_weekly=true&amount=10")

Here's what will happen if we don't meet this goal:
* Marco Rubio will win in Florida and Republicans will keep control of the Senate.
* They'll try to erase our progress. They will jeopardize the rights of women, the LGBT community, and millions of other Americans.
* **AND** they'll decide the future of the Supreme Court.

We can't let that happen. We can beat them, but we need you with us now. As it stands, they're drowning us out with their lies and misleading attacks -- funded by millions of dollars from the Koch brothers, the NRA, and shady dark money groups.

**We need your grassroots support to defeat them, and every single dollar counts.** **All gifts will be MATCHED: Can you give now to win in Florida?** _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | [](https://secure.murphyforflorida.com/page/m/10ae2d9f/738133e4/311f72c7/7f857af6/1353111158/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/pme160930pm2?refcode=pme160930pm2_m&express_lane=true&amount=3")

name: Tracy Newman
subject: Before it’s too late
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jay Inslee for Washington
body:
We're running out of time. [](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=1&ms=OTYxMjA4NAS2&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTAwMjQ2NDQzNAS2&mt=1&rt=0)
| Dear Friend, Time is short, so I'll cut to the chase. We're running out of time to ensure we have enough in the bank to get television ads on the air. Before the end of the day, I need to make decisions. We're $2,715 short of what we need. [**Will you make a donation of $5 right now so we have the funds available?**](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=2&ms=OTYxMjA4NAS2&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTAwMjQ2NDQzNAS2&mt=2&rj=MTAwMjQ2NDQzNAS2&rt=0) It's simple -- we can't buy television airtime on credit or the hope we'll raise the money later. We must have every single dollar in the bank the moment the transfer starts. That's why it's so critical that we hit our goal. Every dollar helps buy us time. Literally. Thank you for pitching in right now, Tracy
Finance Director | [**CONTRIBUTE »**](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=3&ms=OTYxMjA4NAS2&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTAwMjQ2NDQzNAS2&mt=1&rt=0)

clean_body: Derek, On Tuesday, October 4, Governor Mike Pence and Senator Tim Kaine will face off in the vice presidential debate. Join fellow Republicans at VP Debate Watch Parties at several locations across New Hampshire. This will be a great opportunity to watch the debate and volunteer on behalf of the entire GOP ticket. With only 39 days to go, every volunteer shift will make a big difference. Can you join us at one of the below locations? RSVP here. ( ) Tuesday, October 4, 2016 Starts at 7:30PM ET with a phone bank: Keene Cheshire County GOP HQ 222 West Street 03431 Manchester Trump Victory Office 340 Granite St 03102 Nashua GOP Victory Office 11 Main St 03064 Salem Trump Office 303 S Broadway 03079 Stratham GOP Victory Office 62 Portsmouth Ave 03885 Starts at 8:30PM ET: Laconia Fratello’s 799 Union Ave 03246 North Conway The Lobster Trap Restaurant 2840 Westside Rd 03860 We hope to see you there. Please forward to your family, friends, and neighbors! NHGOP Paid for by the New Hampshire Republican State Committee Not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. www.nh.gop ( ) footer image

name: Official Van Hollen Campaign
subject: did you see Barbara Mikulski's email?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Van Hollen for US Senate
body:
Friend, did you see Senator Barbara Mikulski's email?

We need your help to secure the resources Chris needs to purchase air time, launch our final GOTV effort, and increase voter contact across our state. We cannot afford to take anything for granted in the next few weeks. [**Please chip in now >>>**](http://action.vanhollen.org/page/m/2de91aa7/70cdc2ba/47253504/397ae5a/1051242286/VEsH/ "http://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/cvh-keep-fighting?refcode=em093016_2&amount=25.00&recurring=12&utm_medium=email&utm_source=cvanhollen&utm_content=1+-+Please+chip+in+now+gtgtgt&utm_campaign=em093016_2&source=em093016_2")

Begin Forwarded Message:
> |
> Friend--
>
> This election is coming to the decision point. In less than 40 days we will know who our next U.S. Senator is here in Maryland.
>
> We know how crucial it is that we all roll up our sleeves and get involved in making sure that Chris Van Hollen wins.
>
> **We know what's at stake -- the balance in the U.S. Senate, the future of the Supreme Court, the values and priorities of the people of Maryland and much much more.**
>
> That is why I'm doing everything I can to elect Chris. And I'm asking you to do this with me.
>
> Nothing is ever in the bag. We need to band together and work until the polls close to make sure that no one and nothing can take this away from us.
>
> **[Can you chip in right now before tonight's critical fundraising deadline to ensure that Chris' team meets their September goals -- and has what it takes to fight back outside attacks and win big on November 8?](http://action.vanhollen.org/page/m/2de91aa7/70cdc2ba/47253504/397ae5b/1051242286/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/cvh-keep-fighting?refcode=em093016_2&amount=25.00&recurring=12&utm_medium=email&utm_source=cvanhollen&utm_content=2+-+Can+you+chip+in+right+now+before+tonight&utm_campaign=em093016_2&source=em093016_2")**
>
> We have less than six weeks to go -- please join me, and let's get this done.
>
> Barbara Mikulski [DONATE](http://action.vanhollen.org/page/m/2de91aa7/70cdc2ba/47253504/397ae58/1051242286/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/cvh-keep-fighting?refcode=em093016_2&amount=25.00&recurring=12&utm_medium=email&utm_source=cvanhollen&utm_content=3+-+DONATE&utm_campaign=em093016_2&source=em093016_2")
> ---
>

Paid for by Van Hollen for US Senate
---
We believe that emails are a vital way to stay in direct contact with supporters. You're receiving this email at dpwillis67@gmail.com. [**Click here**](http://action.vanhollen.org/page/m/2de91aa7/70cdc2ba/47253504/397ae59/1051242286/VEsC/ "http://action.vanhollen.org/unsubscribe?utm_medium=email&utm_source=cvanhollen&utm_content=4+-+Click+here&utm_campaign=em093016_2&source=em093016_2") if you'd like to unsubscribe from these messages. Please enter dpwillis67@gmail.com to ensure that we are able to unsubscribe you from our list.

We hope to see you there! Sincerely,

Team Trump

Can you believe this week, Friend?

1\. _**MONDAY:**_ Democrats SURGED after Donald Trump's embarrassing debate performance!

2\. _**TUESDAY:**_ We announced a **TRIPLE-MATCH on all gifts** until the FEC end-of-quarter deadline!

3\. _**NOW:**_ With the biggest milestone of this race just hours away, we're on the verge of hitting our goal and dealing the Party of Trump a shocking defeat!
[**Friend, we can 't close the deal without the full support of grassroots Democrats like you. Will you pitch in before the TRIPLE-MATCH expires at midnight?**](https://secure.dscc. "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/dsccab4w?refcode=DPE-20160930-UrgentAlertDS-ProsHV&recurring=auto&recur_weekly=auto&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1= &city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation=&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&amount=5")

We're not exaggerating here: This is our last best chance to defend President Obama's legacy -- and prevent bought-and-paid-for Republicans from turning Donald Trump's ideas into law. **But we don 't get a do-over on this goal, Friend -- if we fall short, our momentum will vanish just like that!**

**It all comes down to what you do right now, Friend. Please answer the call with a QUADRUPLED gift before midnight 's FEC deadline -- we're counting on you!**

**[Pitch in $1 IMMEDIATELY>>](https://secure.dscc. "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/dsccab4w?refcode=DPE-20160930-UrgentAlertDS-ProsHV&recurring=auto&recur_weekly=auto&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation=&amount=1")

Team DSCC

**Democratic Senatorial Campaign Committee
120 Maryland Ave. NE
Washington, DC 20002** Paid for by the DSCC, dscc.org,
and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee.
The DSCC is the only committee solely dedicated to supporting Democrats in the U.S. Senate. This message was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com because you are a part of the DSCC's grassroots network. Please add us to your address book or approved senders list to make sure you keep receiving DSCC updates. Emails are a critical way for us to stay in touch with our grassroots supporters. 99% of the DSCC's contributions come from grassroots donors giving less than $200. We also strive to keep you updated on issues related to the Democratic Party's values.

If you'd like to take a break from DSCC emails, [click here](https://secure.dscc.org/page/m/3954f689/2b07114c/72420ab9/73838e7b/1665364118/VEsHAw/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSJ9/ "https://secure.dscc.org/page/s/take-an-email-break?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com").

Together, we're going to take back the Senate in November, but we'll need the help of every single grassroots supporter to do it. If you'd like to stop receiving all emails from the DSCC, please [click here](https://secure.dscc.org/page/m/3954f689/2b07114c/72420ab9/73838e75/1665364118/VEsHDQ/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSJ9/ "https://secure.dscc.org/page/content/unsubscribe?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com"). body:

name: =?utf-8?Q?Matt=20Donnellan?=
subject: Fwd: they won't outwork us
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Washingtonians for Bryant
body:
|
---
| | Friend

Justin just forwarded me the message below about **tonight's fundraising deadline.** He's exactly right when he says Jay Inslee won't outwork us - we have people knocking on doors and making calls around our state.

I know we'll win if we have the resources to reach people at the door, on television, and online. **[Will you help keep all our voter contact programs fully funded so we can elect Bill Bryant our next governor?](http://billbryantforgovernor.us13.list-manage2.com/track/click?u=a508ffc703e6fc82360a383e8&id=0f32f4db0c&e=32f1a3df98)**

Matt Donnellan
Political Director
Bill Bryant for Governor
(253)220-5051

\---------- Forwarded message ----------
From: Justin Matheson
Date: Fri, Sep 30, 2016 at 12:55 PM
Subject: they won't outwork us
To: mattd@billbryantforgovernor.com

Friend

Tomorrow is our last major end-of-month fundraising deadline of this campaign. How much we raise before tomorrow will determine our plan for the rest of this election.

The Inslee team is raking in millions in contributions from the state Democratic Party, whose platform includes _**a statewide income tax**_.

They will outspend us, but they won’t outwork us. They’re defending an incumbent with a record of mismanagement. Bill made that case at the debate and he won.

Since Bill’s victory Monday, people are paying attention now more than ever. It’s time to get our message back up on TV.

[**Can you help us out by contributing $35, $100, or even $500 towards getting our message out to voters across Washington?**](http://billbryantforgovernor.us13.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=a508ffc703e6fc82360a383e8&id=ed94a3da94&e=32f1a3df98)

Our polling shows our TV ads move voters by more than 18 points – that’s more than Bill needs to win. It’s exactly what Dino Rossi said yesterday; “Bill can win as long as Republicans give him the money he needs to get his message out to voters.” Please help us do that by contributing today.

Justin Matheson
Campaign Manager
Bill Bryant for Governor
(253)220-5051

---
| | | | | | | [](http://billbryantforgovernor.us13.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=a508ffc703e6fc82360a383e8&id=cf4ee99124&e=32f1a3df98)
---

---
| | [](http://billbryantforgovernor.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a508ffc703e6fc82360a383e8&id=0582f21882&e=32f1a3df98)
---

name: Evan Bayh
subject: a big deal
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Evan Bayh Committee, 133 W
body:
| Friend,

The last end-of-quarter deadline before the election is a big deal. The closer we get to November 8th -- and we're pretty close now -- the more important it gets to hit every single one of our fundraising goals, including tonight's.

If we miss it, we risk falling behind, which is dangerous in a race as close as this one.

So I'm coming to you directly with this request: **[Will you chip in $5 today to support my Senate campaign?](https://bayh.bsd.net/page/m/32a504fe/7be36b49/1eb7a94f/78f85cd9/3777721775/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/eb-septeoq?refcode=20160930.2nd&amount=10") Your gift will be matched until midnight.**

I know you're ready to see a change in how the Senate operates. And I'm ready to work with my colleagues to forge the types of common-sense solutions Hoosiers deserve. I won't take orders from anyone but you.

But none of that matters if we end Election Day with a loss. That's why your support matters so much.

The quarter ends tonight, Friend. And this campaign runs because of grassroots donors like you. **[Will you pitch in $5 before midnight and get your gift matched?](https://bayh.bsd.net/page/m/32a504fe/7be36b49/1eb7a94f/78f85cd9/3777721775/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/eb-septeoq?refcode=20160930.2nd&amount=10")**

Thanks for your help,

Evan ** **

********[Contribute](https://bayh.bsd.net/page/m/32a504fe/7be36b49/1eb7a94f/78f85cd8/3777721775/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/eb-septeoq?refcode=20160930.2nd&amount=25")******** ****** ****** ****** ******

name: =?utf-8?Q?Sheena=20Greitens?=
subject: Urgent Request
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Greitens for Missouri, Jeff Stuerman, Treasurer
body:
Peter,
I'm heading home from Branson and I want to share with you how proud I am of what Eric did in the debate this afternoon. I'm proud of the principles he stands for and the solutions he offers our state and our families. The reviews are in and he was the clear winner.

Now Eric needs your help. We're over halfway to our end of the month goal and just need to raise another $12,000 to keep the momentum going. Can you [chip in](http://ericgreitens.us13.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=c81357bdb59fb170d9eb82b3a&id=ddd5a7f3b7&e=84041254e6) so that we can hit our goal?

Our family is committed to doing what's right for families across Missouri. I know Missouri can count on Eric to be the kind of leader we need. Right now, Eric is counting on your support.

Please consider chipping in [$100, $50, $25 or whatever you can](http://ericgreitens.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=c81357bdb59fb170d9eb82b3a&id=34ea845e05&e=84041254e6) right now to help us win this campaign.

Yours,

Sheena Greitens
Paid for by Greitens for Missouri, Jeff Stuerman, Treasurer
4579 Laclede Avenue #138
St. Louis, Missouri 63108
Eric is a former member of the United States Navy having served four deployments overseas. Use of his military rank, titles and photographs in uniform does not imply an endorsement from the Department of the Navy or the Department of Defense.
This email was sent to dpwillis67+greitens@gmail.com. You can also follow us on [Facebook](http://ericgreitens.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=c81357bdb59fb170d9eb82b3a&id=a967a352fa&e=84041254e6) and [Twitter](http://ericgreitens.us13.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=c81357bdb59fb170d9eb82b3a&id=93ebabaccb&e=84041254e6). Emails are one of the best ways to stay connected with the campaign. If they become too much, you can [unsubscribe](http://ericgreitens.us13.list-manage1.com/unsubscribe?u=c81357bdb59fb170d9eb82b3a&id=3f71c40094&e=84041254e6&c=185ccac31c), but please let us know how we can improve. You can always reach us at [team@ericgreitens.com](mailto:team@ericgreitens.com) or (314) 899-0288. You're still reading the fineprint? You must be a detail oriented, all-around awesome person who is a big supporter — you should [volunteer](http://ericgreitens.us13.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=c81357bdb59fb170d9eb82b3a&id=618d181abf&e=84041254e6) with us!

Need you to step up | Friend --

Here’s the reality: we have our last quarterly deadline tonight -- and we are nowhere near our target of $7 million. **[Right here, right now -- I am asking you to step up.](https://b.hrc.onl/eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL3d3dy5oaWxsYXJ5Y2xpbnRvbi5jb20vZG9uYXRlL2dpdmUtdG9kYXkvP3V0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj0yMDE2MDkzMGhmYTNfZnImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZiR3JvdXA9MjcmYk1haWxpbmdJRD0xOTEmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9MTkxLTIwMTYwOTMwLVJNMy1GdWxsJm09MTkxJmVtYWlsPWRwd2lsbGlzNjclNDBnbWFpbC5jb20mdXRtX3NvdXJjZT1iIn0.c25EfdLXxLGm8MIn73sBM1t6mZM3lxqnbEuUItQ5Or0)**

If we don’t reach our goal of $7 million by midnight, we’re going to have to make choices about where our funding goes in key battleground states. Decisions like whether to hire more organizers or cut back, about whether we can afford to set up a new field office in Florida or Ohio or any number of states where we’re tied in the polls.

This is my nightmare: I don’t want to have to leave battleground states understaffed, I don’t want to have to abandon that final TV ad buy, and I don’t want to leave anything about this campaign or this fight to chance. I'm a planner -- we’ve got a strategy with clear action items -- the only known-unknown is whether or not we’ll be able to fund them all.

In order to save us from scaling back this campaign when we should be pushing forward, **we need 117 people to step up from your area right now and help us meet our goal:** [](https://b.hrc.onl/eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL3d3dy5oaWxsYXJ5Y2xpbnRvbi5jb20vZG9uYXRlL2dpdmUtdG9kYXkvP3V0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj0yMDE2MDkzMGhmYTNfZnImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZiR3JvdXA9MjcmYk1haWxpbmdJRD0xOTEmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9MTkxLTIwMTYwOTMwLVJNMy1GdWxsJm09MTkxJmVtYWlsPWRwd2lsbGlzNjclNDBnbWFpbC5jb20mdXRtX3NvdXJjZT1iIn0.c25EfdLXxLGm8MIn73sBM1t6mZM3lxqnbEuUItQ5Or0)
Thanks,

Robby

Hillary for America
